title: rubus moluccanus var. lobbianus
intro: Rubus moluccanus var. lobbianus, also known as Moluccan raspberry or Indian raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus moluccanus plant, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its edible red berries and medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, dysentery, and fever, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus moluccanus var. lobbianus include its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, and its berries can also be eaten fresh or used to make jams and preserves.
